item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My reaction at the end:

"Oh, f*** you." angry face

+1

EDIT: Also, I just realized this thing is Keter and the Foundation doesn't even realize it. Beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Analysis indicates nearly all of SCP-3631's organs have been replaced in this fashion

Should the 3631 here be 3631-2?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey hey! I really liked this and, honestly, think it has a whole bunch of potential. However, it took me a little bit to get past the beginning for one specific reason:

is to be

You use this so much. And I totally understand why. Clinical tone is tough and that phrase just comes naturally when you're trying to keep it. However, it really bogs down the beginning of your SCP. I highly suggest giving the first few paragraphs a once-over and ironing out the repetition.

Otherwise, this is a rather interesting story. I can't help but wonder what the full extent might be but I'm also not sure I want to know, I rather like the mystery! Maybe I'll read your explanation dropdown up there but not right now. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was a clever story, and I also have a soft spot for monsters. I like the idea of an imaginary monster made real, un-made, then re-made and determined to stay alive.

I didn't make the connection about the -1 being the one to rip off -2's arms and legs because of how protective -1 is of -2 in other contexts. As it's written now, it's little bit a mystery how -2 ended up so horribly mangled. But I like it the way it is.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
My entry for Cliché Con 2019. No chance that I'll actually win, but I thought it would be fun to enter anyways. I'm entering under the cliché anomaly "Spooky scary murder monster(s)" since I've always had a soft spot for monster SCPs - even if so few of them are done well - and they seem to have largely fallen out of favor.

+ Explanation
- Expunation

Explaining the story after several requests, and also because I am starting to suspect that people are holding back crit until I do this.

Simon (aka -2, but you probably already knew that) is a reality bender. He doesn't realize it, and he can't control it to any significant degree, but he is. As a kid, he unknowingly created a monster (aka -1), which the Foundation then contains. Simon gets the concussion and forgets about the monster, causing it to go *POOF* and disappear from containment without a trace.

Fast forward, and Simon, now an adult, is shown some of his pre-concussion drawings by his mother. This brings the monster back to life. Fearful of being forgotten again, the monster captures Simon and severs his limbs so he can't escape or fight back. The monster then tries replacing his organs with those of children, thinking it will make him a kid again. But it won't. There's no going back.

There's some more details I have in my headcanon (the relationship between kid Simon and the monster specifically is something I didn't get the chance to explore here), but this is all that can be deduced with what evidence exists on the page. Everything I just explained is hinted at in some way.

Thanks to Salamander724, Scented_Shadow, JayrassicPark, Ouran-OS, Zyn, The Great Hippo, aismallard, and probably some other people I'm forgetting (really need to start writing this stuff down earlier) for crit!
